title: Add Google site verification file for Search Console

requirements.text: Create a Google site verification HTML file at frontend/public/googleXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXX.html (using a placeholder filename that follows Google's standard verification file naming pattern, e.g. google-site-verification.html) that contains the standard Google verification meta tag content. Since the actual verification code must be provided by the user from Google Search Console, add a clearly named placeholder file at frontend/public/google-site-verification.html with instructional content explaining that the user must replace this file with the actual file downloaded from Google Search Console (https://search.google.com/search-console), which will have a filename like 'googleXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXX.html' and content like 'google-site-verificationXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXX'.

quotes: upload to my site for google verification

acceptanceCriteria: A file exists at frontend/public/google-site-verification.html that is served as a static asset
The file is accessible at the /google-site-verification.html route when the app is deployed
The file contains clear instructions for the site owner to replace it with the actual Google-provided verification file
The instructions reference the Google Search Console URL (https://search.google.com/search-console) and explain the typical file naming pattern (googleXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXX.html)
The SeoInfoBanner on the Home page is updated to mention uploading the Google verification file as a step in the Search Console submission process

constraints: Do not modify any files under frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, frontend/src/main.tsx, or frontend/src/components/ui
The verification file must be placed in frontend/public so it is served as a static asset at the root URL path
nonGoals: Hardcoding a specific Google verification token (the actual token must come from the site owner's Search Console account)
Automating the Google Search Console submission process
